title: BenchmarkDiff payload
description: Emitted by measure.bench_run on every completed run (success OR error). Records the per-metric delta against the stored baseline so consumers can chart drift over time without re-reading the harness output. The chain-close-completeness audit + future trained perf-regression-classifier consume this. On error (subprocess timeout, parse failure), metrics is empty + error names the failure mode.

metrics.description: Per-metric diff entries. Empty array when the harness errored before producing parseable output.

baseline.description: The baseline value from baseline_json_path. Numeric metrics carry numbers; non-numeric carry strings; absent baselines carry null.

observed.description: The observed value from the just-ran harness output. Numeric / string / null per metric shape.

delta_abs.description: observed - baseline for numeric metrics; null for non-numeric or when either side is null.

delta_pct.description: (observed - baseline) / baseline * 100 for numeric non-zero baselines; null otherwise.

run_latency_ms.description: Wall-clock latency of the harness subprocess run in milliseconds. Matches timeout_ms exactly when the run timed out (the field doubles as the timeout-fired signal alongside the error field).

baseline_updated.description: True when the call was invoked with update_baseline=true; the diff is zero by construction in that case (baseline overwritten before computing). False on the normal compare-against-existing-baseline path.

stderr_log_path.description: Optional repo-absolute path to the subprocess stderr log (/tmp/bench-<slug>-<ts>.stderr.log). Set when the run produced any stderr output.

error.description: Optional error string when the subprocess failed (timeout, non-zero exit, parse failure). Empty / absent on success.

gate_passed.description: Deterministic-metric gate verdict. Absent/null when the harness declared no gate_metrics (report-only) or on the error paths (gate not computed). True when every metric matching the harness's gate_metrics allowlist had an exact-zero delta; false when a gate metric drifted (or the patterns matched no metrics). Lets a re-run produce a meaningful pass/fail even when non-gate metrics (latency, live-data envelope sizes) drift. See bug bench-diff-exact-equality-vs-live-data-defeats-regression-detection.

gate_failures.description: Names (with baseline/observed values) of the gate metrics that drifted, or a single misconfiguration line when gate_metrics matched no metrics. Empty/absent when the gate passed or wasn't configured.
